content:
In Danilo's Allocator patch series, I stumbled on the section ordering Safety and then Examples, I also had this come up in the past and I had the following response then:

I think we should standardize that Invariants and Safety sections
are put last. This is because people reading the HTML version are
probably not interested in the inner workings. This also makes it
possible to have the invariants and the struct definition on the same
screen.

I want to create a good-first-issue for this. How do we want to sort the other section headers? My initial idea is this:

  • # Examples
  • # Panics
  • # Invariants
  • # Guarantees
  • # Safety

I also saw that in the old rust branch we had the Errors section header (we also have some others) from vendored alloc, do we want to "revive" that? (ie use it to document when a function returns Err)

The idea for doing this is the following:

  • make safety documentation immediately visible for people viewing the source code (eg when using an LSP to jump to the definition)
  • for people browsing the HTML or viewing the documentation via an LSP, the Examples section comes first, so it's easy to get started with the API
any opinions on this? (if you agree that I should create the issue, then just react with :+1:)
I would say that outside of the kernel, it's pretty normal for examples to be the last section.
do you know any reasons for that?
Well, the text at the beginning and sections such as panics or errors are explanations of how the API behaves. The examples are then a supplement to anyone who wants more help.
Sure, we can put Panics above Examples. what about the safety docs though?
Also part of the APIs specification.
I would say that Invariants are not part of the API
Guarantees and Safety yes
Agreed.
I would prefer Safety before examples. It would be bad to miss.
hmm my argument is that it becomes easy to miss if it is not the last section, since when you look at the code, then you might miss it
but I guess that we want to cater to people that read the docs

How about this:?

  • # Safety
  • # Guarantees
  • # Panics
  • # Errors
  • # Examples
  • # Invariants
I don't think we need to enforce an ordering more precisely than saying "these go before examples and these go after examples".
panics before errors? shrug, probably depends on the precise case what makes more sense
I like the idea of enforcing an ordering to get consistency across the board.
I don't have an opinion on the exact ordering though. I think what you posted looks good.
A good first issue for this sounds good to me!
created #1116
I haven't tagged it as good-first-issue yet, will do so in a couple days if nobody shouts
